subject: Sociology
title: Religion and Social Capital
description: One-minute skill: Religion and Social Capital.

hook.text: Need a job? A babysitter? A loan? In many communities, the first place to ask is the congregation.

buildup.text: Robert Putnam found that religious communities are America's largest source of social capital and networks.

discovery.text: Churchgoers volunteer more, give more to charity, and report more trust — even outside religious causes.

twist.text: It's the community, not the theology. Secular groups with similar structure produce the same benefits.

climax.text: Religion's social power isn't supernatural. It's organizational. Regular gatherings build trust and reciprocity.

punchline.text: Showing up together builds social wealth.

quiz.question: What did Putnam find about religious communities?
options: They are the largest source of social capital
